targetSlots.planningTest: Show what the protagonist wants, lacks, or cannot tolerate before the story changes.
Present the concrete path into the story problem.
End the opening with a choice or consequence that prevents easy retreat.
Reveal information or opposition that changes what the protagonist thinks the story is.
Confront the protagonist with the flaw, false belief, or missing knowledge.
Make victory depend on a meaningful choice, not only force or luck.

concept.premise: Orin Vale, a failed apprentice archivist, steals a fireproof index from the Ember Library and discovers that every banned book is a sleeping witness. To clear his sister's treason charge, he must wake the right witness before the library burns its own memories.
concept.readerPromise: A library-heist fantasy adventure about forbidden knowledge, family loyalty, and the danger of waking truths that remember you.
concept.centralConflict: Orin needs forbidden testimony to save his sister, while the Archivist Council protects the library by destroying witnesses that implicate them.

protagonist.name: Orin Vale
protagonist.desire: restore his apprentice rank and save his condemned sister
protagonist.fear: proving his sister guilty after risking everything for her
protagonist.flaw: he hides behind clever thefts instead of asking people to trust him

characters.name: Sable Reed
characters.materiality: A rival index thief who knows the library's secret routes and charges painful favors.

characters.name: Mira Vale
characters.materiality: Orin's accused sister knows which banned book was planted but refuses to name her informant.

characters.name: Warden Cass
characters.materiality: The council's fire warden can burn memories, records, and rooms before testimony wakes.

worldFacts.fact: An ember index points to banned books only when the seeker names the person they are willing to betray.
Banned books sleep as witnesses and wake speaking in the voice of their last honest reader.
Library fire burns remembered events before it burns paper, removing evidence from witnesses and suspects.

storyPromise.text: If Orin wakes the right book, he can prove who framed Mira but may erase someone else's innocence.

constraints: Produce planning contracts only, not prose.
Use scene contracts as the generation and adherence unit.
Make character and world obligations materially affect choices, costs, or consequences.
Keep the diagnostic to six chapter-contract units.
